sentences.sentence: Chelsea Rochman, an ecologist at the University of California, Davis, has been trying to answer a dismal question: Is everything terrible, or are things just very, very bad?
sentences.translation: 加州大学戴维斯分校的生态学家切尔西·罗克曼一直试图回答一个令人沮丧的问题：一切都很糟糕，还是情况只是非常、非常糟？

structure.text: an ecologist at the University of California, Davis
has been trying to answer a dismal question
Is everything terrible, or are things just very, very bad

difficult_points: an ecologist... 是 Chelsea Rochman 的同位语，补充人物身份。
has been trying 是现在完成进行时，强调她持续进行的研究努力。
冒号后是 a dismal question 的具体内容，由两个选择疑问分句构成。
very, very 通过重复加强语气，表示“极其糟糕”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 回答一个令人沮丧的问题

sentences.sentence: Rochman is a member of the National Center for Ecological Analysis and Synthesis's marine-debris working group, a collection of scientists who study, among other things, the growing problem of marine debris, also known as ocean trash.
sentences.translation: 罗克曼是国家生态分析与综合中心海洋垃圾工作组的成员；这个工作组由一群科学家组成，他们研究的问题包括日益严重的海洋废弃物问题，也就是海洋垃圾。

structure.text: is a member of the National Center for Ecological Analysis and Synthesis's marine-debris working group
a collection of scientists who study, among other things, the growing problem of marine debris
who study, among other things, the growing problem of marine debris

difficult_points: 长主干是 Rochman is a member of...，后面多个短语补充说明工作组性质。
a collection of scientists 是 marine-debris working group 的同位语。
among other things 是插入语，表示研究内容不止海洋垃圾。
also known as ocean trash 是过去分词短语，解释 marine debris 的通俗名称。

expressions.meaning_zh: 除其他事项外；尤其包括

sentences.sentence: Plenty of studies have sounded alarm bells about the state of marine debris; in a recent paper published in the journal Ecology, Rochman and her colleagues set out to determine how many of those perceived risks are real.
sentences.translation: 大量研究已经就海洋垃圾的状况敲响警钟；在最近发表于《生态学》期刊的一篇论文中，罗克曼和同事着手确定这些被认为存在的风险中有多少是真实的。

structure.text: have sounded alarm bells about the state of marine debris
in a recent paper published in the journal Ecology
set out to determine how many of those perceived risks are real
how many of those perceived risks are real

difficult_points: 分号连接两个相关分句：已有研究警告风险，Rochman 团队检验风险是否真实。
have sounded alarm bells 是现在完成时，表示已有大量研究发出警示。
published in the journal Ecology 是过去分词短语，修饰 paper。
how many... are real 是 determine 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: Often, Rochman says, scientists will end a paper by speculating about the broader impacts of what they've found.
sentences.translation: 罗克曼说，科学家往往会在论文结尾推测其发现可能产生的更广泛影响。

structure.text: will end a paper by speculating about the broader impacts of what they've found
by speculating about the broader impacts of what they've found

difficult_points: Rochman says 是插入语，主干是 scientists will end a paper...
by speculating... 表示结束论文的方式。
what they've found 是介词 of 的宾语从句，指研究发现。
broader impacts 表示超出具体实验结果的更广泛影响。

sentences.sentence: For example, a study could show that certain seabirds eat plastic bags, and go on to warn that whole bird populations are at risk of dying out.
sentences.translation: 例如，一项研究可能显示某些海鸟会吃塑料袋，并继续警告说整个鸟类种群都有灭绝风险。

structure.text: could show that certain seabirds eat plastic bags
that certain seabirds eat plastic bags
and go on to warn that whole bird populations are at risk of dying out
that whole bird populations are at risk of dying out

difficult_points: could show 和 go on to warn 是同一主语 a study 的并列谓语。
两个 that 从句分别作 show 和 warn 的宾语。
go on to warn 表示在展示具体发现之后进一步提出警告。
are at risk of dying out 表示面临灭绝风险。

sentences.sentence: 'But the truth was that nobody had yet tested those perceived threats,' Rochman says.
sentences.translation: 罗克曼说：“但事实是，还没有人检验过那些被认为存在的威胁。”

structure.text: that nobody had yet tested those perceived threats
had yet tested those perceived threats

difficult_points: that 引导表语从句，说明 truth 的具体内容。
had yet tested 是过去完成时，表示到当时为止尚未完成检验。
nobody 与 yet 搭配形成否定意义：还没有任何人做过验证。
句末 Rochman says 是引述语，说明话语来源。

expressions.meaning_zh: 检验被认为存在的威胁

sentences.sentence: 'There wasn't a lot of information.'
sentences.translation: “相关信息并不多。”
difficult_points: There wasn't 是存在句的否定形式，表示“没有很多……”。
a lot of information 是真正表达存在与否的信息内容。
引号表明这是 Rochman 对前一句的补充说明。

sentences.sentence: Rochman and her colleagues examined more than a hundred papers on the impacts of marine debris that were published through 2013.
sentences.translation: 罗克曼和她的同事审查了一百多篇截至 2013 年发表的关于海洋垃圾影响的论文。

structure.text: examined more than a hundred papers on the impacts of marine debris
on the impacts of marine debris

difficult_points: more than a hundred papers 是 examined 的宾语，表示研究样本规模。
on the impacts of marine debris 修饰 papers，说明论文主题。
that were published through 2013 是定语从句，限定论文发表时间范围。
through 2013 表示“截至 2013 年”。

sentences.sentence: Within each paper, they asked what threats scientists had studied - 366 perceived threats in all - and what they'd actually found.
sentences.translation: 在每篇论文中，他们询问科学家研究了哪些威胁——总共 366 个被认为存在的威胁——以及他们实际发现了什么。

structure.text: asked what threats scientists had studied

difficult_points: Within each paper 表示分析单位是每一篇论文。
what threats... 和 what they'd actually found 是 asked 后的两个并列宾语从句。
破折号中的 366 perceived threats in all 是对 threats 的数量补充。
had studied 与 had found 都是过去完成时，回看论文作者此前完成的研究。

sentences.sentence: In 83 percent of cases, the perceived dangers of ocean trash were proven true.
sentences.translation: 在 83% 的案例中，海洋垃圾被认为存在的危害被证明是真实的。

structure.text: the perceived dangers of ocean trash

difficult_points: In 83 percent of cases 给出比例范围，是理解结论力度的关键信息。
perceived dangers 指此前只是被认为存在、尚待验证的危害。
were proven true 是被动语态，表示这些危害经过研究验证为真。

sentences.sentence: In the remaining cases, the working group found the studies had weaknesses in design and content which affected the validity of their conclusions—they lacked a control group, for example, or used faulty statistics.
sentences.translation: 在其余案例中，工作组发现这些研究在设计和内容上存在弱点，影响了其结论的有效性——例如，它们缺少对照组，或使用了有缺陷的统计方法。

structure.text: found the studies had weaknesses in design and content
the studies had weaknesses in design and content
which affected the validity of their conclusions
they lacked a control group, for example, or used faulty statistics

difficult_points: found 后省略 that，引出宾语从句 the studies had weaknesses...
which 引导定语从句，修饰 weaknesses in design and content。
em dash 后具体举例说明研究弱点。
lacked 和 used 是并列谓语，说明两类可能的问题。

sentences.sentence: Strikingly, Rochman says, only one well-designed study failed to find the effect it was looking for, an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits.
sentences.translation: 罗克曼说，引人注目的是，只有一项设计良好的研究未能发现它所寻找的影响，那是一项关于贻贝摄入微小塑料碎片的调查。

structure.text: failed to find the effect it was looking for
an investigation of mussels ingesting microscopic plastic bits

difficult_points: Strikingly 是评论性副词，提示这一发现出人意料。
only one well-designed study 是主语，强调数量非常少。
it was looking for 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 effect。
an investigation... 是同位语，说明这项研究的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: The plastic moved from the mussels' stomachs to their bloodstreams, scientists found, and stayed there for weeks—but didn't seem to stress out the shellfish.
sentences.translation: 科学家发现，塑料从贻贝的胃移动到它们的血流中，并在那里停留数周——但似乎并没有让这些贝类产生压力。

structure.text: moved from the mussels' stomachs to their bloodstreams
but didn't seem to stress out the shellfish

difficult_points: moved... and stayed... 是 The plastic 的并列谓语。
scientists found 是插入语，说明发现来源。
em dash 后用 but 引出与预期相反的结果：没有明显造成压力。
stress out 是动词短语，表示使贝类产生压力。

sentences.sentence: While mussels may be fine eating trash, though, the analysis also gave a clearer picture of the many ways that ocean debris is bothersome.
sentences.translation: 不过，尽管贻贝吃垃圾可能没什么问题，这项分析也更清楚地展示了海洋垃圾令人困扰的许多方式。

structure.text: While mussels may be fine eating trash
also gave a clearer picture of the many ways

difficult_points: While 引导让步从句，承认贻贝案例似乎问题不大。
though 是插入性转折标记，加强让步语气。
gave a clearer picture of 表示“更清楚地展示”。
that ocean debris is bothersome 修饰 ways，说明海洋垃圾造成困扰的方式。

sentences.sentence: Within the studies they looked at, most of the proven threats came from plastic debris, rather than other materials like metal or wood.
sentences.translation: 在他们查看的研究中，大多数已被证实的威胁来自塑料垃圾，而不是金属或木头等其他材料。

structure.text: Within the studies they looked at
rather than other materials like metal or wood

difficult_points: Within the studies... 限定结论来自他们审查的研究范围。
they looked at 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 studies。
rather than 引出对比对象，强调主要威胁来自塑料而非其他材料。
proven threats 指已经被研究证实的威胁。

sentences.sentence: Most of the dangers also involved large pieces of debris—animals getting entangled in trash, for example, or eating it and severely injuring themselves.
sentences.translation: 大多数危险还涉及大块垃圾——例如，动物被垃圾缠住，或者吃下垃圾并严重伤害自己。

structure.text: also involved large pieces of debris
or eating it and severely injuring themselves

difficult_points: em dash 后的内容举例说明 large pieces of debris 带来的危险。
animals getting entangled... 是独立结构，表示动物被垃圾缠住的情形。
eating it and severely injuring themselves 是两个并列动作。
it 指代 trash 或 debris。

sentences.sentence: But a lot of ocean debris is 'microplastic', or pieces smaller than five millimeters.
sentences.translation: 但许多海洋垃圾是“微塑料”，也就是小于五毫米的碎片。

structure.text: or pieces smaller than five millimeters

difficult_points: But 转向另一类垃圾：前文讨论大块垃圾，这里讨论微塑料。
or pieces smaller than five millimeters 是对 microplastic 的解释性同位语。
smaller than five millimeters 是比较结构，给出微塑料的尺寸标准。

sentences.sentence: These may be ingredients used in cosmetics and toiletries, fibers shed by synthetic clothing in the wash, or eroded remnants of larger debris.
sentences.translation: 这些可能是化妆品和洗漱用品中使用的成分、合成衣物洗涤时脱落的纤维，或较大垃圾侵蚀后留下的残片。

structure.text: may be ingredients used in cosmetics and toiletries, fibers shed by synthetic clothing in the wash, or eroded remnants of larger debris
shed by synthetic clothing in the wash

difficult_points: These 指上一句的 microplastic pieces。
may be 后接三个并列名词短语，列出微塑料来源。
used in... 和 shed by... 都是过去分词短语，分别修饰 ingredients 与 fibers。
eroded remnants 表示被侵蚀后留下的残片。

sentences.sentence: Compared to the number of studies investigating large-scale debris, Rochman's group found little research on the effects of these tiny bits.
sentences.translation: 与研究大型垃圾的研究数量相比，罗克曼的团队发现关于这些微小碎片影响的研究很少。

structure.text: Compared to the number of studies investigating large-scale debris
found little research on the effects of these tiny bits
on the effects of these tiny bits

difficult_points: Compared to... 是过去分词短语，用于建立比较对象。
investigating large-scale debris 后置修饰 studies。
little research 表示研究数量很少，带有否定含义。
these tiny bits 指前文的 microplastic。

sentences.sentence: 'There are a lot of open questions still for microplastic,' Rochman says, though she notes that more papers on the subject have been published since 2013, the cutoff point for the group's analysis.
sentences.translation: 罗克曼说：“关于微塑料仍有许多悬而未决的问题。”不过她指出，自 2013 年——该团队分析的截止点——以来，关于这一主题的论文已经发表了更多。

structure.text: There are a lot of open questions still for microplastic
though she notes that more papers on the subject have been published since 2013
that more papers on the subject have been published since 2013
the cutoff point for the group's analysis

difficult_points: There are 是存在句，说明关于微塑料仍有许多问题未解。
though 引导让步从句，补充说明 2013 年后已有更多研究发表。
have been published 是现在完成时被动语态，强调截止点之后的研究进展。
the cutoff point... 是 2013 的同位语，解释为何提到该年份。

sentences.sentence: There are also, she adds, a lot of open questions about the ways that ocean debris can lead to sea-creature death.
sentences.translation: 她补充说，关于海洋垃圾如何导致海洋生物死亡，也还有许多悬而未决的问题。

structure.text: a lot of open questions about the ways
that ocean debris can lead to sea-creature death

difficult_points: There are 是存在句，真正的信息核心是 a lot of open questions。
she adds 是插入语，说明这是 Rochman 的补充观点。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 ways。
lead to sea-creature death 表示导致海洋生物死亡的机制。

sentences.sentence: Many studies have looked at how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal's tissues or cells, rather than whole populations.
sentences.translation: 许多研究关注的是塑料如何影响单个动物，或该动物的组织或细胞，而不是整个种群。

structure.text: have looked at how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal's tissues or cells
how plastic affects an individual animal, or that animal's tissues or cells

difficult_points: have looked at 是现在完成时，概括既有研究的关注点。
how 引导 look at 的宾语从句，说明研究内容。
or that animal's tissues or cells 补充 individual animal 的更小层级。
rather than whole populations 与前面的个体、组织、细胞形成对比。

sentences.sentence: And in the lab, scientists often use higher concentrations of plastic than what's really in the ocean.
sentences.translation: 而且在实验室中，科学家常常使用比海洋中实际浓度更高的塑料浓度。

structure.text: often use higher concentrations of plastic
than what's really in the ocean

difficult_points: And 延续上一句，增加既有研究局限的另一点。
higher... than... 构成比较结构，比较实验室浓度与真实海洋浓度。
what's really in the ocean 是 than 后的名词性从句。
concentrations of plastic 指塑料在实验环境中的浓度。

sentences.sentence: None of that tells us how many birds or fish or sea turtles could die from plastic pollution—or how deaths in one species could affect that animal's predators, or the rest of the ecosystem.
sentences.translation: 这些都不能告诉我们有多少鸟类、鱼类或海龟可能因塑料污染而死亡，也不能告诉我们某一物种的死亡会如何影响该动物的捕食者或生态系统的其余部分。

structure.text: how many birds or fish or sea turtles could die from plastic pollution
or how deaths in one species could affect that animal's predators, or the rest of the ecosystem
could affect that animal's predators, or the rest of the ecosystem

difficult_points: None of that 概括前文提到的个体层面研究和实验室高浓度设置。
tells us 后接两个 how 引导的并列宾语从句。
em dash 前后连接两个尚未回答的生态层面问题。
that animal's predators 和 the rest of the ecosystem 是 affect 的并列宾语。

sentences.sentence: 'We need to be asking more ecologically relevant questions,' Rochman says.
sentences.translation: 罗克曼说：“我们需要提出更多与生态相关的问题。”

structure.text: need to be asking more ecologically relevant questions

difficult_points: need to be asking 使用进行式，强调应持续提出这类问题。
ecologically relevant 修饰 questions，表示问题应与生态系统层面的影响相关。
Rochman says 是引述语，说明观点来源。

sentences.sentence: Usually, scientists don't know exactly how disasters such as a tanker accidentally spilling its whole cargo of oil and polluting huge areas of the ocean will affect the environment until after they've happened.
sentences.translation: 通常，科学家并不确切知道像油轮意外泄漏全部石油货物并污染大片海域这样的灾难会如何影响环境，直到这些灾难发生之后才知道。

structure.text: don't know exactly how disasters such as a tanker accidentally spilling its whole cargo of oil and polluting huge areas of the ocean will affect the environment
how disasters such as a tanker accidentally spilling its whole cargo of oil and polluting huge areas of the ocean will affect the environment
such as a tanker accidentally spilling its whole cargo of oil and polluting huge areas of the ocean

difficult_points: how 引导 know 的宾语从句，说明科学家不知道的具体内容。
such as... 举例说明 disasters，其中 spilling 和 polluting 是并列现在分词。
will affect the environment 是 how 从句中的谓语部分。
until after they've happened 表示只有灾难发生后才能准确了解影响。

sentences.sentence: 'We don't ask the right questions early enough,' she says.
sentences.translation: 她说：“我们没有足够早地提出正确的问题。”

structure.text: don't ask the right questions early enough

difficult_points: the right questions 与上一句 ecologically relevant questions 呼应。
early enough 表示时间不够提前，是作者认为研究不足的关键。
she says 是引述语，其中 she 指 Rochman。

sentences.sentence: But if ecologists can understand how the slow-moving effect of ocean trash is damaging ecosystems, they might be able to prevent things from getting worse.
sentences.translation: 但如果生态学家能够理解海洋垃圾的缓慢影响如何正在损害生态系统，他们也许就能防止情况恶化。

structure.text: if ecologists can understand how the slow-moving effect of ocean trash is damaging ecosystems
how the slow-moving effect of ocean trash is damaging ecosystems
might be able to prevent things from getting worse

difficult_points: But 引出转折：尽管很多问题提出得太晚，慢性影响仍可能提前理解。
if 引导条件从句，主句说明可能产生的积极结果。
how... is damaging ecosystems 是 understand 的宾语从句。
prevent things from getting worse 中 from 后接动名词 getting。

sentences.sentence: Asking the right questions can help policy makers, and the public, figure out where to focus their attention.
sentences.translation: 提出正确的问题可以帮助政策制定者以及公众弄清楚应把注意力集中在哪里。

structure.text: can help policy makers, and the public, figure out where to focus their attention
figure out where to focus their attention

difficult_points: Asking the right questions 是动名词短语作主语。
help 后接宾语 policy makers, and the public，再接不带 to 的 figure out。
where to focus their attention 是疑问词加不定式结构，作 figure out 的宾语。
policy makers 与 the public 并列，说明受益对象不只科学家。

sentences.sentence: The problems that look or sound most dramatic may not be the best places to start.
sentences.translation: 那些看起来或听起来最戏剧化的问题，未必是最适合开始处理的地方。

structure.text: The problems that look or sound most dramatic
that look or sound most dramatic
may not be the best places to start

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句，修饰 problems。
look or sound 是并列系动词，表示表面呈现方式。
may not be 表示可能不是，语气较谨慎。
the best places to start 是比喻表达，指最适合优先处理的问题。

expressions.meaning_zh: 看起来或听起来最戏剧化

sentences.sentence: For example, the name of the 'Great Pacific Garbage Patch'—a collection of marine debris in the northern Pacific Ocean—might conjure up a vast, floating trash island.
sentences.translation: 例如，“大太平洋垃圾带”这个名称——指北太平洋中的一片海洋垃圾集合——可能会让人联想到一座巨大的漂浮垃圾岛。

structure.text: the name of the 'Great Pacific Garbage Patch'
a collection of marine debris in the northern Pacific Ocean
might conjure up a vast, floating trash island

difficult_points: For example 引出关于公众误解的具体例子。
em dash 中的 a collection... 是 Great Pacific Garbage Patch 的解释性同位语。
might conjure up 表示“可能唤起某种想象”，不是实际存在。
a vast, floating trash island 是公众可能形成的夸张图像。

sentences.sentence: In reality though, much of the debris is tiny or below the surface; a person could sail through the area without seeing any trash at all.
sentences.translation: 然而实际上，大部分垃圾很小或位于水面以下；一个人可能驶过这片区域，却根本看不到任何垃圾。

structure.text: is tiny or below the surface
could sail through the area without seeing any trash at all
without seeing any trash at all

difficult_points: In reality though 与上一句可能唤起的垃圾岛想象形成转折。
分号连接两个说明真实情况的分句。
below the surface 与 tiny 并列，说明垃圾不易被肉眼看到的原因。
without seeing any trash at all 表示即使穿过该区域也可能完全看不到垃圾。

sentences.sentence: A Dutch group called 'The Ocean Cleanup' is currently working on plans to put mechanical devices in the Pacific Garbage Patch and similar areas to suck up plastic.
sentences.translation: 一个名为“The Ocean Cleanup”的荷兰组织目前正在制定计划，准备在太平洋垃圾带及类似区域放置机械装置来吸走塑料。

structure.text: A Dutch group called 'The Ocean Cleanup'
to put mechanical devices in the Pacific Garbage Patch and similar areas

difficult_points: called 'The Ocean Cleanup' 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 A Dutch group。
is currently working on 使用现在进行时，表示当前正在推进计划。
to put... 修饰 plans，说明计划内容。
to suck up plastic 是目的不定式，说明机械装置的用途。

sentences.sentence: But a recent paper used simulations to show that strategically positioning the cleanup devices closer to shore would more effectively reduce pollution over the long term.
sentences.translation: 但最近一篇论文使用模拟显示，从长期来看，将清理装置策略性地布置在离海岸更近的地方会更有效地减少污染。

structure.text: that strategically positioning the cleanup devices closer to shore would more effectively reduce pollution over the long term
strategically positioning the cleanup devices closer to shore
would more effectively reduce pollution over the long term

difficult_points: But 转折，指出清理装置未必应放在垃圾带中心。
used simulations to show 表示使用模拟来证明或展示结论。
that 引导 show 的宾语从句，从句主语是动名词短语 strategically positioning...
over the long term 表示从长期效果来看。

sentences.sentence: 'I think clearing up some of these misperceptions is really important,' Rochman says.
sentences.translation: 罗克曼说：“我认为澄清其中一些误解非常重要。”

structure.text: clearing up some of these misperceptions is really important
clearing up some of these misperceptions

difficult_points: I think 后省略 that，引出宾语从句。
clearing up... 是动名词短语，在从句中作主语。
these misperceptions 指前文关于海洋垃圾形态和治理重点的误解。
Rochman says 是引述语，说明观点来源。

sentences.sentence: Among scientists as well as in the media, she says, 'A lot of the images about strandings and entanglement and all of that cause the perception that plastic debris is killing everything in the ocean.'
sentences.translation: 她说，在科学家以及媒体中，“许多关于搁浅、缠绕以及诸如此类情况的图像造成了一种印象，即塑料垃圾正在杀死海洋中的一切。”

structure.text: Among scientists as well as in the media
A lot of the images about strandings and entanglement and all of that
that plastic debris is killing everything in the ocean
is killing everything in the ocean

difficult_points: Among scientists as well as in the media 指这种认知存在于科学界和媒体中。
she says 插在句中，主干在引号内。
A lot of the images... 是主语，核心词是 images。
that 引导同位语从句，解释 perception 的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: Interrogating the existing scientific literature can help ecologists figure out which problems really need addressing, and which ones they'd be better off—like the mussels—absorbing and ignoring.
sentences.translation: 审问式地梳理现有科学文献，可以帮助生态学家弄清哪些问题确实需要处理，哪些问题则更适合像贻贝那样吸收并忽略。

structure.text: and which ones they'd be better off—like the mussels—absorbing and ignoring

difficult_points: Interrogating... 是动名词短语作主语，表示系统质询现有文献。
help ecologists figure out 后接两个 which 引导的并列宾语从句。
need addressing 中 addressing 是动名词，表示“需要被处理”。
em dash 中的 like the mussels 插入类比，呼应前文贻贝吸收塑料但似乎无碍的案例。

expressions.meaning_zh: 最好做某事；做某事更合适
